section.heading: Safeguards
section.chapeau: The head of each Federal agency shall establish safeguards against the removal or loss of records the head of such agency determines to be necessary and required by regulations of the Archivist. Safeguards shall include making it known to officials and employees of the agency—

paragraph.content: that records in the custody of the agency are not to be alienated or destroyed except in accordance with sections 3301–3314 of this title, and
the penalties provided by law for the unlawful removal or destruction of records.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: Based on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., § 396(e) (June 30, 1949, ch. 288, title V, § 506(e), as added Sept. 5, 1950, ch. 849, § 6(d), 64 Stat. 583).

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Sections 3304 to 3307 of this title, included in the reference in par. (1) to sections 3301 to 3314 of this title, were repealed by Pub. L. 91–287, § 2(c), June 23, 1970, 84 Stat. 321.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2014—Pub. L. 113–187 substituted “the head of such agency” for “he” in introductory provisions.
1984—Pub. L. 98–497 substituted “Archivist” for “Administrator of General Services”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1984 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 98–497 effective Apr. 1, 1985, see section 301 of Pub. L. 98–497, set out as a note under section 2102 of this title.
